title: Bridge Networks, DNS, Ports, Routing, IPv6, and Exposure Policy
description: A multi-service application works through localhost on one machine but fails by service name and accidentally exposes its database to every interface. Learners cumulatively produce least-exposure container network topology through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries.
Use user-defined networks, embedded DNS, service names, aliases, attach/detach behavior, and IP churn without hard-coded addresses.
Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.
